services:
  - id: svc_001
    name: "Odoo CE 19"
    category: erp
    hosting: azure_container_apps
    edge: azure_front_door
    endpoint: "erp.insightpulseai.com"
    status: active
    promotion_state: live
    health_check: "https://erp.insightpulseai.com/web/health"

  - id: svc_002
    name: "n8n Automation"
    category: automation
    hosting: azure_vm_transition
    endpoint: "n8n.insightpulseai.com"
    status: decommissioned
    promotion_state: deleted
    decommissioned: "2026-03-25"
    note: "VM and DNS deleted. Workflows migrating to Foundry agents + DevOps pipelines."

  - id: svc_003
    name: "Supabase (Self-Hosted)"
    category: database
    hosting: azure_vm
    project_ref: "spdtwktxdalcfigzeqrz"
    status: decommissioned
    promotion_state: deleted
    decommissioned: "2026-03-25"
    note: "VM deleted. Auth → Entra, data → Databricks, functions → ACA/Foundry."

  - id: svc_004
    name: "Auth Gateway (Keycloak)"
    category: identity
    hosting: azure_container_apps
    endpoint: "auth.insightpulseai.com"
    aca_name: ipai-auth-dev
    status: decommissioned
    promotion_state: deleted
    decommissioned: "2026-03-25"
    note: "Never operationalized. ACA app deleted. Entra ID is canonical IdP."

  - id: svc_005
    name: "MCP Coordinator"
    category: orchestration
    hosting: azure_container_apps
    edge: azure_front_door
    endpoint: "mcp.insightpulseai.com"
    aca_name: ipai-mcp-dev
    status: active
    promotion_state: live
    migrated_from_do: "2026-03-11"
    note: "Stub (nginx:alpine). Replace with MCP gateway image when ready."

#

# =============================================================================
# Operating Model — InsightPulse AI
# =============================================================================
#
# Defines tool authority boundaries between Azure DevOps and GitHub.
# Answers: "Which tool owns what?" for planning, code, CI/CD, and deployment.
#
# Cross-references:
#   - azdo-project-repo-alignment.yaml (AzDo project → repo mapping)
#   - repo-azure-alignment.yaml (repo → Azure resource mapping)
#   - ADR-GSR-001 (goals SoR decision)
#   - .claude/rules/github-governance.md (GitHub Apps, PR discipline)
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/github/features-timeline
#   - https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/devops/plan/scaling-agile
# =============================================================================

schema_version: "1.0"
schema: ssot.governance.operating-model.v1
last_updated: "2026-03-17"

organization: insightpulseai

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tool Authority Matrix
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Each tool has exactly one primary role. No overlap in authority.

tool_authority:

to authenticate requests to ocr.insightpulseai.com.
      Minimum 32 random bytes. Generate with:
        python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
      Set as BEARER_TOKEN env var on the ocr-prod container and as
      ir.config_parameter in Odoo. Referenced in ssot/runtime/prod_settings.yaml.

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Plane.so integration credentials
  # scope: runtime — consumed by supabase/functions/plane-sync and plane-webhook
  # See: ssot/integrations/plane_webhooks.yaml, docs/architecture/PLANE_APP_CONNECTOR.md
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  plane_api_key:
    purpose: "Plane.so API key (X-API-Key) for server-side sync worker"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: plane_api_key
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env    # server-only; never expose to browser
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:plane_api_key   # consumed by supabase/functions/plane-sync
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding or Plane workspace credential reset"
    notes: >
      Plane API key (X-API-Key header). Server-side only.
      Obtain from: Plane workspace → Settings → API Tokens.
      See: ssot/integrations/plane_webhooks.yaml

  plane_oauth_client_id:
    purpose: "Plane OAuth2 app client ID for user-scoped access"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: plane_oauth_client_id
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:plane_oauth_client_id
    rotation_policy: "On Plane app re-registration"
    notes: "Pair with plane_oauth_client_secret. See: docs/architecture/PLANE_APP_CONNECTOR.md"

  plane_oauth_client_secret:
    purpose: "Plane OAuth2 app client secret for token exchange"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: plane_oauth_client_secret
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:plane_oauth_client_secret
    rotation_policy: "On Plane app re-registration or exposure"
    notes: "Pair with plane_oauth_client_id. NEVER expose in browser or logs."

  plane_workspace_id:
    purpose: "Plane.so workspace slug — injected into all API paths, MCP tool calls, and sync workers"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: plane_workspace_id
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:plane_workspace_id
    rotation_policy: "On workspace rename or access revocation"
    notes: >
      Workspace slug (not UUID) used in Plane REST API paths: /api/v1/workspaces/{slug}/...
      Obtain from: Plane → Settings → General → Workspace Slug.
      See: ssot/integrations/plane.yaml, ssot/integrations/plane_mcp.yaml

  plane_slack_client_id:
    purpose: "Slack Client ID for Plane's native Slack integration (self-hosted)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
    consumers:
      - plane:self_hosted
    rotation_policy: "On Plane Slack app re-registration"
    notes: >
      Configured in Plane self-hosted instance environment (PLANE_SLACK_CLIENT_ID).
      Obtained from: Slack API → Your Apps → Plane app → Basic Information → Client ID.
      Part of the two-app Slack model (see ssot/integrations/slack/apps.yaml).
      This is Plane's app — separate from Pulser's Slack credentials.

  plane_slack_client_secret:
    purpose: "Slack Client Secret for Plane's native Slack integration (self-hosted)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
    consumers:
      - plane:self_hosted
    rotation_policy: "On Plane Slack app re-registration or secret exposure"
    notes: >
      Configured in Plane self-hosted instance environment (PLANE_SLACK_CLIENT_SECRET).
      Obtained from: Slack API → Your Apps → Plane app → Basic Information → Client Secret.
      NEVER log or echo. Part of the two-app Slack model.

  plane_webhook_secret:
    purpose: "Shared secret for validating inbound Plane webhook payloads"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
      - vercel_env
    vault_secret_name: plane_webhook_secret
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:plane_webhook_secret
      - vercel_env:apps/ops-console  # PLANE_WEBHOOK_SECRET for webhook signature verification
      - platform:apps/ops-console/app/api/webhooks/plane/route.ts
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding or exposure"
    notes: >
      Embedded as X-Plane-Webhook-Secret header or in webhook URL path.
      Generate: python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
      See: ssot/integrations/plane_webhooks.yaml §risk_flags.webhook_no_signature
      Contract: docs/contracts/C-PLANE-02-workitems-webhooks.md
      SSOT: ssot/sources/plane/work_items.yaml (secret_ref: plane_webhook_secret)

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # n8n automation runner credentials
  # scope: runtime — consumed by n8n self-hosted instance and CI automation
  # See: ssot/integrations/n8n.yaml, docs/architecture/N8N_AUTOMATION_CONTRACT.md
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  n8n_api_key:
    purpose: "n8n REST API key for external triggering (CI scripts, agents, GitHub Actions)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: n8n_api_key
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # n8n API key must not be injected into CI env directly
      - vercel_env       # server-side only
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:n8n_api_key
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding or n8n credential reset"
    notes: >
      Used to trigger n8n workflows programmatically via the REST API
      (e.g., POST /api/v1/workflows/{id}/execute).
      Set as the value of the X-N8N-API-KEY header.
      Obtain from: n8n → Settings → API → Create API key.
      See: ssot/integrations/n8n.yaml, docs/architecture/N8N_AUTOMATION_CONTRACT.md §6

  n8n_webhook_secret:
    purpose: "Shared HMAC/Header-Auth secret for n8n webhook node authentication"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: n8n_webhook_secret
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:n8n_webhook_secret
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "Rotate on team member offboarding or webhook endpoint leak"
    notes: >
      Used as the secret value in n8n Header Auth credentials attached to
      webhook trigger nodes. Callers must include the header X-N8N-Webhook-Secret
      (or the credential-specific header name) in every webhook POST.
      Minimum 32 random bytes. Generate with:
        python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
      Also configure as the corresponding caller-side header in:
        apps/slack-agent (for Slack → n8n callback paths, if any)
        GitHub webhook payloads via X-Hub-Signature-256 (separate secret)
      See: automations/n8n/CREDENTIALS.md

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # n8n self-hosted authentication secrets
  # scope: runtime — n8n editor basic auth on DO Droplet
  # See: ssot/n8n/self_hosted.yaml
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  n8n_basic_auth_user:
    purpose: "n8n editor UI basic auth username"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
      - platform
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      runtime: platform              # Docker env var on DO Droplet
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env
    consumers:
      - platform:deploy/odoo-prod.compose.yml
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Basic auth username for the n8n editor at n8n.insightpulseai.com.
      Set via N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER env var in Docker Compose.

  n8n_basic_auth_password:
    purpose: "n8n editor UI basic auth password"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
      - platform
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      runtime: platform
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions
      - vercel_env
    consumers:
      - platform:deploy/odoo-prod.compose.yml
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Basic auth password for the n8n editor. Minimum 16 characters.
      Set via N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD env var in Docker Compose.
      Generate with: python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(24))"

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Supabase partner integrations secrets
  # scope: runtime/infra — Cloudflare Workers edge runtime, Vercel hosting
  # See: ssot/integrations/cloudflare_workers.yaml, ssot/integrations/vercel_hosting.yaml
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  vercel_token:
    key: vercel_token
    service: vercel
    scope: infra
    purpose: >
      Vercel API token for CI automation (only if needed for programmatic deploys
      via Vercel REST API). Omit entirely if Vercel deploys are manual or
      GitHub-linked (push-to-deploy) only.
    required_for:
      - vercel_deployments
      - project_env_sync
    approved_stores:
      - github_actions
      - os_keychain
    github_secret_name: VERCEL_TOKEN
    vault_path: "infra/vercel/token"
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: github_actions_secret
      runtime: prohibited    # Vercel token must not be injected into Vercel env vars (recursive)
    prohibited_consumers:
      - vercel_env           # Recursive — do not inject the Vercel token into Vercel itself
      - supabase_vault       # Not needed at runtime; infra-only
    consumers:
      - github_actions:.github/workflows/deploy-vercel.yml
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "Annually or on team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Omit entirely if Vercel deploys are manual/GitHub-linked only (push-to-deploy).
      Only provision this token when CI needs to trigger or manage Vercel deployments
      programmatically (e.g., via `vercel deploy --token`).
      Obtain from: Vercel Dashboard → Settings → Tokens.
      See: ssot/integrations/vercel_hosting.yaml

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Figma design platform credentials
  # scope: runtime — consumed by supabase/functions/figma-*, CI variables sync,
  #   MCP agent bridge, Code Connect publish
  # See: ssot/integrations/figma.yaml, docs/architecture/FIGMA_INTEGRATION_SURFACE.md
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  figma_oauth_client_id:
    purpose: "Figma OAuth2 app client ID for user-scoped REST API access"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: figma_oauth_client_id
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # inject via Vault read, not direct env injection
      - vercel_env       # browser-facing; must not reach browser
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:figma_oauth_client_id   # consumed by supabase/functions/figma-api
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "On Figma app credential reset or team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Pair with figma_oauth_client_secret.
      Obtain from: Figma → Settings → Apps → OAuth2 app registration.
      Use OAuth2 for user-scoped access (design team CI, workspace reads).
      For internal tooling (CI, MCP, agents) prefer figma_personal_access_token.
      See: ssot/integrations/figma.yaml, docs/architecture/FIGMA_INTEGRATION_SURFACE.md §3

  figma_oauth_client_secret:
    purpose: "Figma OAuth2 app client secret for token exchange (server-only)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: figma_oauth_client_secret
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # server-only; must not appear in CI env vars
      - vercel_env       # must never reach browser
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:figma_oauth_client_secret   # consumed by supabase/functions/figma-api
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "On Figma app credential reset or team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Server-only. Token exchange must happen in supabase/functions/figma-api/ only.
      NEVER log or echo this value; rotate immediately on exposure.
      Pair with figma_oauth_client_id.
      See: ssot/integrations/figma.yaml, docs/architecture/FIGMA_INTEGRATION_SURFACE.md §3

  figma_personal_access_token:
    purpose: "Figma PAT for internal automation (CI, MCP server bridge, agent tooling)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: figma_personal_access_token
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # inject via Vault read at runtime, not as CI env var directly
      - vercel_env       # server-only; must not reach browser
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:figma_personal_access_token   # consumed by MCP bridge + figma-api
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding or suspected exposure"
    notes: >
      PAT has no built-in expiry — rotate proactively on offboarding.
      Prefer OAuth2 for user-scoped access; use PAT only for internal tooling
      (CI variables sync, MCP agent bridge, Code Connect publish).
      Obtain from: Figma → Account Settings → Personal access tokens.
      Used by agents/mcp/figma.json as FIGMA_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN env var.
      See: ssot/integrations/figma.yaml, docs/architecture/FIGMA_INTEGRATION_SURFACE.md §3, §7

  figma_webhook_secret:
    purpose: "Shared secret for Figma webhook signature verification (x-figma-signature HMAC-SHA256)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
    vault_secret_name: figma_webhook_secret
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # server-only; must not be in CI env
      - vercel_env       # must never reach browser
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:figma_webhook_secret   # consumed by supabase/functions/figma-webhook
      - os_keychain:local-dev
    rotation_policy: "On webhook endpoint compromise or team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Must be set BEFORE registering any Figma webhook via the API.
      supabase/functions/figma-webhook/ verifies x-figma-signature on every request.
      Function refuses to start if this secret is absent from Vault (cold-start guard).
      Minimum 32 random bytes. Generate with:
        python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
      Set as the 'passcode' field in the webhook registration payload.
      See: ssot/integrations/figma.yaml, docs/architecture/FIGMA_INTEGRATION_SURFACE.md §4

# ── Vercel AI Gateway credentials ─────────────────────────────────────────────

  vercel_ai_gateway_api_key:
    purpose: "Vercel AI Gateway API key — unified LLM provider routing + observability/billing."
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - vercel_env        # server-side only (process.env, never client-side)
    vault_secret_name: vercel_ai_gateway_api_key
    storage_policy:
      local: vercel_env (pulled via `vercel env pull`)
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: vercel_env (server-side only)
    prohibited_consumers:
      - browser_bundle    # must never reach client-side bundle
      - github_actions    # route through Supabase Vault
    consumers:
      - vercel_env:apps/workspace        # RAG endpoint LLM calls
      - vercel_env:apps/slack-agent      # agent LLM calls
      - vercel_env:apps/odooops-console  # Advisor AI explanations
    rotation_policy: "On suspected key exposure or quarterly review."
    notes: >
      Single endpoint for many LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) with unified
      billing and observability. Use to route model calls instead of calling providers directly.
      Log each call to ops.run_events with provider/model/cost metadata.
      See: ssot/integrations/vercel_ai_gateway.yaml, docs/architecture/VERCEL_AI_SURFACES.md

  cron_secret:
    purpose: >
      Vercel Cron job authorization secret. Vercel injects
      "Authorization: Bearer ${CRON_SECRET}" on every scheduled cron invocation.
      Route handlers validate this header before executing cron logic.
    approved_stores:
      - vercel_env
    vercel_env_name: CRON_SECRET
    consumers:
      - vercel_env:apps/ops-console        # /api/cron/tick, /api/cron/advisor-nightly
      - scripts:scripts/vercel/env_sync.ts # synced via env_sync --project odooops-console
    rotation_policy: "Annually or on suspected token exposure"
    notes: >
      Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
      Apply via: CRON_SECRET=<value> pnpm tsx scripts/vercel/env_sync.ts --project odooops-console --env production
      Or manually: vercel env add CRON_SECRET production
      Consumers: apps/ops-console/app/api/cron/tick/route.ts,
                 apps/ops-console/app/api/cron/advisor-nightly/route.ts
      See: apps/ops-console/vercel.json (crons schedule), docs/runbooks/CRON_JOBS.md

  vercel_token:
    purpose: "Vercel personal access token — project settings PATCH, env var sync, deployment triggers"
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
    consumers:
      - scripts:scripts/vercel/apply_project_settings.sh
      - scripts:scripts/vercel/env_sync.ts
    rotation_policy: "Annually or on team member offboarding"
    notes: >
      Local development only. Never commit. Never inject into CI as VERCEL_TOKEN (use
      GitHub Actions secret or Vercel's own CI integration instead).
      Real token stored in Vercel CLI auth.json:
        ~/Library/Application Support/com.vercel.cli/auth.json
      Set for scripts: export VERCEL_TOKEN=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('$HOME/Library/Application Support/com.vercel.cli/auth.json'))['token'])")
      See: infra/vercel/projects/ops-console.json

# ── Mailgun secrets (mail catcher + transactional) ────────────────────────────

  mailgun_signing_key:

    vault_secret_name: mailgun_signing_key
    consumers:

    rotation_policy: "On compromise or Mailgun key rotation."

      ONLY for non-prod mail catcher (smtp.mailgun.org:2525) and E2E smoke tests.
      Never inject into prod Odoo ir.mail_server config.
      SSOT: ssot/odoo/mail.yaml § non_production

  odoo_mailgun_smtp_password:
    purpose: >
      Mailgun SMTP password for the production Odoo ir.mail_server record
      (ipai_mailgun_smtp module, smtp.mailgun.org:2525).
      Injected at Odoo install/upgrade by post_install_hook via
      ODOO_MAILGUN_SMTP_PASSWORD env var. Never seeded in XML data files.
    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain
      - supabase_vault
    vault_secret_name: odoo_mailgun_smtp_password
    env_var_name: ODOO_MAILGUN_SMTP_PASSWORD
    consumers:
      - odoo:addons/ipai/ipai_mailgun_smtp/hooks.py    # injected at install time
    rotation_policy: "Annually or on Mailgun account credential reset."
    notes: >
      Distinct from mailgun_smtp_password (non-prod catcher) — same Mailgun
      account but separate secret entry to enforce prod/non-prod isolation.
      Set in the Odoo server environment before running:
        odoo -i ipai_mailgun_smtp   (first install)
        odoo -u ipai_mailgun_smtp   (after rotation)
      SSOT: ssot/odoo/mail.yaml § production

# ── GitHub webhook receiver secret ────────────────────────────────────────────
  github_webhook_secret:
    purpose: "Shared HMAC-SHA256 secret for verifying inbound GitHub webhook payloads (X-Hub-Signature-256)"
    scope: runtime
    approved_stores:
      - supabase_vault
      - os_keychain
      - vercel_env
    vault_secret_name: github_webhook_secret
    storage_policy:
      local: os_keychain
      ci: supabase_vault
      runtime: supabase_vault
    prohibited_consumers:
      - github_actions   # server-only; must not appear in CI env vars
    consumers:
      - supabase_vault:github_webhook_secret   # consumed by ops-console webhook receiver
      - vercel_env:apps/ops-console  # GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET for server-side Next.js API route (not browser)
      - platform:apps/ops-console/app/api/webhooks/github/route.ts
    rotation_policy: "On team member offboarding or GitHub webhook endpoint compromise"
    notes: >
      Used to verify X-Hub-Signature-256 headers on inbound GitHub issue webhooks.
      Format: sha256=<lowercase hex of HMAC-SHA256 over raw request body>.
      Minimum 32 random bytes. Generate with:
        python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
      Set as the 'secret' field when registering the webhook in GitHub repository settings.
      Vercel env var is server-side only (Next.js API route — never exposed to browser).
      Contract: docs/contracts/C-GH-02-workitems-webhooks.md
      SSOT: ssot/sources/github/work_items.yaml (secret_ref: github_webhook_secret)

# =============================================================================
# GitHub App credentials — registered 2026-03-02
# Context: spec/github-integrations/prd.md §FR-4
# App:     pulser-hub (Insightpulseai org)
# Previously undocumented in SSOT despite active use in github-app-auth function.
# =============================================================================

  github_app_id:
    key: github_app_id
    type: config               # numeric App ID; not a secret but tracked for SSOT completeness
    owner: platform
    purpose: >
      Numeric GitHub App ID for pulser-hub. Used to construct the JWT audience
      claim when minting installation tokens in github-app-auth Edge Function.
      Not a secret — visible in the App's public URL.
    consumers:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_id
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_APP_ID    # available to CI workflows that call github-app-auth
    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_id
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_APP_ID
    rotation:
      cadence: "never"         # changes only if the App is re-created
      signal: manual
    healthcheck:
      kind: none
    status:
      desired: present
      severity_if_missing: high

  github_app_private_key:
    key: github_app_private_key
    type: private_key          # RSA PEM — critical secret
    owner: platform
    purpose: >
      RSA private key for the pulser-hub GitHub App. Used to sign JWTs that are
      exchanged for short-lived (1h) GitHub installation tokens. This key is the
      only long-lived credential in the GitHub App identity chain.
    consumers:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_private_key
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY   # referenced by github-app-auth Edge Function
    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_private_key
      - kind: keychain
        name: github-app-pulser-hub-private-key
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY
    rotation:
      cadence: "365d"
      signal: "GitHub App key rotation — revoke old key first, then add new"
    healthcheck:
      kind: http
      probe:
        system: github
        endpoint: "/app"
        auth: jwt               # signed with this key; probe verifies key is valid
    prohibited_consumers:
      - vercel_env             # never in Vercel — supabase function only
    status:
      desired: present
      severity_if_missing: critical

  github_app_client_id:
    key: github_app_client_id
    type: config               # OAuth client ID; not a secret but tracked for SSOT
    owner: platform
    purpose: >
      OAuth App client ID for pulser-hub GitHub App. Used in OAuth flow when
      end-users authorize ops-console to act on their behalf. Not a secret.
    consumers:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_client_id
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_CLIENT_ID
    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_client_id
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_CLIENT_ID
    rotation:
      cadence: "never"
      signal: manual
    healthcheck:
      kind: none
    status:
      desired: present
      severity_if_missing: medium

  github_app_client_secret:
    key: github_app_client_secret
    type: secret               # OAuth client secret
    owner: platform
    purpose: >
      OAuth client secret for pulser-hub GitHub App. Used to exchange OAuth
      authorization codes for user access tokens. Only needed if OAuth (user
      login via GitHub) is active. For automation-only use cases this can
      remain not_provisioned.
    consumers:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_client_secret
      - kind: github_secret
        name: GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET
    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_client_secret
      - kind: keychain
        name: github-app-pulser-hub-client-secret
    rotation:
      cadence: "365d"
      signal: manual
    healthcheck:
      kind: none
    prohibited_consumers:
      - vercel_env             # server-side only; never browser-exposed
    status:
      desired: present
      severity_if_missing: medium

  github_app_webhook_secret:
    key: github_app_webhook_secret
    type: secret
    owner: platform
    purpose: >
      HMAC-SHA256 shared secret set on the pulser-hub GitHub App webhook.
      Used by ops-github-webhook-ingest to verify X-Hub-Signature-256 headers
      on all inbound GitHub event payloads. Distinct from github_webhook_secret
      (which covers repo-level webhooks); this is the App-level webhook secret.
      See: ssot/github/app-manifest.yaml §webhook.secret_ref
    consumers:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_webhook_secret
    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_webhook_secret
      - kind: keychain
        name: github-app-pulser-hub-webhook-secret
    rotation:
      cadence: "365d"
      signal: "Update GitHub App webhook secret + supabase vault simultaneously"
    healthcheck:
      kind: http
      probe:
        system: supabase
        endpoint: "/rest/v1/rpc/vault_secret_exists"
        auth: bearer
    prohibited_consumers:
      - vercel_env             # edge function only; never in Next.js
      - github_actions         # server-only; must not appear in CI env vars
    status:
      desired: present
      severity_if_missing: critical

  github_app_id_n8n:
    key: github_app_id_n8n

    healthcheck:

    status:
      desired: not_provisioned

  github_app_webhook_secret_n8n:

    storage:
      - kind: supabase_vault
        name: github_app_webhook_secret_n8n
      - kind: keychain

      cadence: "365d"
      signal: "Update GitHub App webhook secret + supabase vault simultaneously"

      desired: not_provisioned

  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    approved_stores:
      - os_keychain

      - supabase_vault:AUTONOMA_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID

      desired: not_provisioned

    consumers:

# This file seeds Azure Boards when ADR-GSR-001 graduation gates pass.
#
# Native hierarchy (Basic process): Epic → Issue → Task
#   - Epic = strategic objective / thematic grouping
#   - Issue = scoped requirement or workstream (NOT "Feature" — Basic process
#     uses "Issue" as the middle tier, equivalent to Feature in Agile/Scrum)
#   - Task = concrete deliverable unit
#
# Planning/coordination layer: Delivery Plans + Area Paths + Iteration Paths
#                              + Dashboards + Dependency Links
#
# Azure Boards is the execution coordination system of record for strategic
# objectives, scoped workstreams, concrete tasks, team ownership, iteration
# cadence, area ownership, dependency visibility, Delivery Plans, portfolio
# rollups, dashboards/reporting, and timeline views. Detailed specs,
# architecture doctrine, SSOT files, and runtime evidence remain in the repo.
#
# Constraint: Boards hold the goal record, execution hierarchy, accountable
# owner, measurable acceptance, and links. Full PRDs, architecture doctrine,
# target-state SSOT, and module contracts stay in spec/, docs/architecture/,
# and ssot/ — never in Boards.

# =============================================================================
# DNS Subdomain Registry — Single Source of Truth
# =============================================================================
# This file is the CANONICAL source for all subdomains under insightpulseai.com.
# All DNS configuration is generated from this file.
# DNS authority: Azure DNS (nameservers delegated from Squarespace).
# Cloudflare is deprecated as of 2026-03-26 — Azure DNS is authoritative.
#
# Generation: ./scripts/generate-dns-artifacts.sh
# Validation: .github/workflows/dns-sync-check.yml (CI enforces sync)
# Authority contract: ssot/infrastructure/dns_mail_authority.yaml
#
# DO NOT manually edit generated files:
#   - docs/architecture/runtime_identifiers.json
#   - infra/dns/dns-validation-spec.json
# =============================================================================
#
# lifecycle field (schema: ssot.dns.v2)
# ─────────────────────────────────────
# active     → Terraform provisions/maintains the record.
#              REQUIRED: provider_claim.status must be "claimed" with claimed_at + claim_ref.
#              CI gate (check_dns_provider_claims.py) blocks merge if not satisfied.
#
# planned    → Record is defined in SSOT but Terraform SKIPS it.
#              Safe to commit; no DNS change occurs.
#              Optional: set expires_at to auto-age stale planned records.
#              CI gate fails if expires_at is in the past (forces resolution).
#
# deprecated → Record was once active but is scheduled for removal.
#              Terraform may retain it temporarily; no new features should depend on it.
#              Must have deprecated_date and removal_target fields.
#
# provider_claim field (schema: ssot.dns.provider_claim.v1)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Required on ALL subdomains (active and planned alike).
#
#   provider:    WHO owns/manages the target endpoint.
#                Allowed: azure | other
#                Deprecated: cloudflare | vercel | digitalocean
#
#   status:      Claim state.
#                Allowed: unclaimed | claimed
#
#   claimed_at:  YYYY-MM-DD — REQUIRED when status=claimed.
#
#   claim_ref:   String — REQUIRED when status=claimed.
#                  "ssot:azure-front-door"         → Azure Front Door CNAME
#                  "ssot:azure-dns"                → Azure DNS direct record
#
#   expires_at:  YYYY-MM-DD — OPTIONAL, only meaningful on planned records.
#
# =============================================================================
# POLICY NOTES (2026-03-26)
# =============================================================================
# - DNS authority: Azure DNS (NS delegated from Squarespace). Cloudflare retired.
# - Registrar: Squarespace (NS delegation only, no record edits).
# - Mail: Zoho (service only). MX/SPF/DKIM/DMARC records in Azure DNS.
# - DigitalOcean is fully decommissioned. No A records may point to 178.128.112.214.
# - Vercel is deprecated. Mailgun is deprecated. Supabase is deprecated.
# - Azure Front Door is the canonical public ingress for all platform apps.
# - Front Door endpoint: ipai-fd-dev-ep-fnh4e8d6gtdhc8ax.z03.azurefd.net
# - Apex (insightpulseai.com) serves the standalone website, NOT a redirect to ERP.
# =============================================================================

# Module hierarchy

The target architecture consolidates from 109 modules down to 60, organized in 8 layers. Each layer has a clear responsibility boundary.

## Architecture principles

1. **Config, then OCA, then Delta.** Use built-in configuration first, OCA second, custom `ipai_*` only when needed.
2. **Thin extensions.** Prefer inheriting existing Odoo models over creating new ones.
3. **Single responsibility.** One module addresses one domain concern.
4. **Export analytics.** Heavy analytics belong in Supabase/ADLS, not Odoo.

## Layer summary

| Layer | Count | Purpose | Key modules |
|-------|-------|---------|-------------|
| **Core** | 5 | Base dependencies, workspace, branding, tenancy, feature flags | `ipai_dev_studio_base`, `ipai_workspace_core`, `ipai_ce_branding`, `ipai_tenant_core`, `ipai_module_gating` |
| **Platform** | 7 | Theme, audit, approvals, workflow, permissions, design tokens, icons | `ipai_platform_theme`, `ipai_platform_audit`, `ipai_platform_approvals`, `ipai_platform_workflow`, `ipai_platform_permissions`, `ipai_ui_brand_tokens`, `ipai_web_icons_fluent` |

[options]
; =============================================================================
; Odoo Production Environment Configuration
; =============================================================================
; Extended Platform Configuration for Odoo 19 CE + OCA
; Environment: PRODUCTION
; Database: odoo

; Environment label — read by hooks via RUNNING_ENV env var (Docker / systemd).
; This setting documents intent for human operators; hooks use the env var.
running_env = prod

; Database
db_host = postgres
db_port = 5432
db_user = odoo
db_password = odoo
db_name = odoo
dbfilter = ^odoo$
db_sslmode = require

; Addons Path — immutable, image-baked addon roots (no runtime code mounts)
; Layout: /opt/odoo/src/oca/<repo>/ per OCA repo, /opt/odoo/src/ipai/, /opt/odoo/src/local/
; Each OCA repo root directly contains module directories (Odoo does not recurse).
; This line is overwritten at image build time by Dockerfile.ce19 from
; /etc/odoo/addons_path.generated (dynamically enumerated from repos on disk).
; SSOT: config/addons.manifest.yaml
addons_path = /usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/odoo/addons,/opt/odoo/src/ipai,/opt/odoo/src/local

; Server
http_port = 8069
proxy_mode = True
; Explicit base URL — prevents auto-detection from ACA internal FQDN
web.base.url = https://erp.insightpulseai.com
workers = 4
max_cron_threads = 2

; Performance
limit_memory_hard = 2684354560
limit_memory_soft = 2147483648
limit_request = 8192
limit_time_cpu = 600
limit_time_real = 1200
limit_time_real_cron = -1

; Logging
log_level = info
log_handler = :INFO
logfile = /var/log/odoo/odoo.log

; Security
list_db = False
admin_passwd = False

; Outgoing Mail — Zoho SMTP over 587/TLS (Azure-safe, port 25 is blocked)
; Credentials resolved at runtime via Azure Key Vault + managed identity.
; SSOT: config/odoo/mail_settings.yaml
; No username/password in this file — injected as env vars at runtime.
smtp_server = smtppro.zoho.com
smtp_port = 587
smtp_ssl = False
smtp_starttls = True
email_from = no-reply@insightpulseai.com

#   4. Run: python scripts/odoo/validate_addons_manifest.py
#   5. Commit all changed files together
# =============================================================================

    authoritative_system: supabase
    adls_layers: [bronze, silver]
    reverse_etl_allowed: false

  - entity: account.move
    authoritative_system: odoo
    adls_layers: [bronze, silver, gold]
    reverse_etl_allowed: false
    note: "Posted entries are immutable. Never writable from external systems."

  - entity: project.project
    authoritative_system: odoo
    adls_layers: [bronze, silver, gold]
    reverse_etl_allowed: true
    reverse_etl_mode: enrichment_writeback
    reverse_etl_columns: ["x_forecast_amount", "x_forecast_date"]

# SSOT: Tenancy Model
# Cross-referenced by: docs/architecture/TENANCY_MODEL.md
# Updated: 2026-03-24

version: "1.0.0"

tenant_identifiers:
  customer_tenant_id:
    scope: business_tenant
    description: Maps to a customer org / Odoo company
    example: cust_abc123
    note: Primary business boundary. Never conflate with entra_tenant_id.

  entra_tenant_id:
    scope: identity_plane
    description: Microsoft Entra directory identifier
    example: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
    note: Identity only. Not a business tenant key.

  workspace_id:
    scope: creative_workspace
    description: Studio / creative workspace boundary
    example: ws_studio_001

  environment_id:
    scope: deployment_environment
    description: dev / staging / prod
    allowed_values:
      - dev
      - staging
      - prod

  instance_id:
    scope: runtime_instance
    description: Service instance within an environment
    example: ipai-odoo-dev-web

isolation_planes:
  public:
    tenant_awareness: none
    surfaces:
      - marketing_landing
      - public_docs
      - public_assistant_advisory
    rule: No tenant data. No customer_tenant_id in request context.

  multitenant_control_plane:
    tenant_awareness: logical
    surfaces:
      - diva_copilot_orchestration
      - platform_metadata
      - governance_observability
    rule: All queries include customer_tenant_id. Cross-tenant leakage is P0.

  tenant_isolated_execution:
    tenant_awareness: isolated
    surfaces:
      - odoo_erp_runtime
      - odoo_copilot
      - document_intelligence
      - genie_analytics
    rule: Company-scoped. No cross-company data in single response.

  tenant_isolated_creative:
    tenant_awareness: isolated
    surfaces:
      - w9_studio
      - studio_copilot
    rule: Workspace-scoped. Brand presets and assets do not leak.
